summary: Great soundboard recording (though a bit muffled at times) of an electrifying 10‑16‑89 show with standout Dark Star, Help>Slip>Franklin’s and a jam‑packed second set.
review: The SBD capture of the 10/16/89 Meadowlands gig is generally clear and balanced, even if a few listeners note occasional muffling. Musically it shines – the first set is tight and song‑rich while the second set rolls nonstop with monster Dark Star, Help>Slip>Franklin’s, Attics, Playin’ reprises and a fiery Let It Grow→Deal sequence.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews overwhelmingly praise the performance (many 5‑star) with only a handful noting poor sound; weighted average of show (≈4.7) and recording (≈3.5) yields ~4.3.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most listeners describe the SBD as clear, with Brent’s keys audible and better than the CD release. A few note muffled sections or occasional drop‑outs, but overall the mix is balanced and listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is tight and song‑rich (Picasso Moon → Half Step → Stranger, etc.), second set flows without breaks, building a continuous jam marathon that peaks with Dark Star and the Help>Slip>Franklin’s sequence.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone on Dark Star is praised as “chilling” and his improvisational attack/echo delay work is highlighted.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is noted as solid groove‑wise, though some CD mixes make Phil sound light; the SBD restores his presence.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s birthday show is celebrated – pink polo shirt, vocal fills on Let It Grow>Deal, and overall energetic rhythm work.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland’s keyboards are described as clear in the dub, comfortable with the band, and a key part of the new‑face era.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming gets mixed notes – “Drums>Space” called uninspired by one reviewer, but overall the rhythm section is praised for staying tight through the nonstop second set.
